title: The Starvel Hollow Tragedy
description: The Starvel Hollow Tragedy — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "The Starvel Hollow Tragedy" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: alibi
memes.description: In criminal investigation and law, an alibi (from Latin alibi, meaning "elsewhere") is a defense whereby a suspect proves they were in a different location at the time a crime was committed, making their involvement physically impossible.

memes.label: Annecy
memes.description: Annecy is a town in the Haute-Savoie department of southeastern France, situated at the northern end of Lac d'Annecy.

memes.label: arson
memes.description: Arson is the criminal act of deliberately and maliciously setting fire to property — buildings, vehicles, or land.

memes.label: Autopsy
memes.description: Post-mortem medical examination in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, a forensic procedure that Inspector French argues is essential to establishing the true cause of death beyond clinical presumption.

memes.label: beira-bank
memes.description: The Beira Bank is a fictional Brazilian financial institution based in Rio de Janeiro.

memes.label: blackmail
memes.description: Blackmail is the criminal act of demanding payment or other benefit from a person in return for not revealing compromising or damaging information about them.

memes.label: body-snatching
memes.description: Body-snatching, in its strict legal sense, is the unlawful removal of a corpse from its grave.

memes.label: braeside
memes.description: Braeside is a small detached house situated about half a mile from the town of Kintilloch, Fifeshire, and "a few hundred yards from the gate of the Ransome Institute" [006-012].

memes.label: carter-and-stephenson
memes.description: Carter & Stephenson is a firm of safe manufacturers based in Leeds, makers of safe number 12,473 — the safe installed at Starvel Hollow in which Simon Averill kept his cash, including the £1,400 in bank notes that were supposedly destroyed in the fire [ch04].

memes.label: Certainty
memes.description: The epistemological state between suspicion and proof in French's investigative method — "suspicion grow to certainty and certainty to proof"

memes.label: Chance
memes.description: The interplay of chance and design in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, where apparent coincidences conceal criminal intention and investigative success depends on recognising the pattern beneath randomness.

memes.label: chief-inspector-mitchell
memes.description: Chief Inspector Mitchell is a senior officer at Scotland Yard and the immediate superior of Inspector Joseph French.

memes.label: circumstantial-evidence
memes.description: Circumstantial evidence is indirect proof that relies on inference to connect it to a conclusion of fact.

memes.label: clue
memes.description: A clue is a piece of evidence or information that guides an investigation toward the discovery of facts.

memes.label: Coincidence
memes.description: The role of apparent coincidence in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, where multiple deaths, timely absences, and chance encounters form a pattern that resists purely accidental explanation.

memes.label: "Colophon"
memes.description: "Standard Ebooks colophon"

memes.label: confession
memes.description: A confession is a voluntary statement by a person acknowledging their commission of a crime or other wrongdoing.

memes.label: coroner
memes.description: In The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, the coroner — Dr.

memes.label: criminal-investigation-department
memes.description: The Criminal Investigation Department (CID) is the detective branch of the Metropolitan Police, operating from New Scotland Yard in London.

memes.label: Cumulative Evidence
memes.description: Inspector French's investigative philosophy in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy — the method of building proof through gradual accretion of verified, interlocking facts rather than single dramatic revelations.

memes.label: "Dedication"
memes.description: "Book dedication"

memes.label: Deduction
memes.description: French's method of logical deduction from observed facts — "analysis and deduction" as the core of his investigative technique

memes.label: detective-department
memes.description: The Detective Department of the Edinburgh police is a Scottish law enforcement division that assists Inspector French by providing background information on the death of Flora Roper, John Roper's wife, in Kintilloch, Fife [007-029].

memes.label: disguise
memes.description: In The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, disguise refers to the deliberate alteration of one's physical appearance to conceal identity.

memes.label: Doubt
memes.description: The role of doubt as an investigative tool, narrative device, and moral question in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, where Inspector French transforms uncertainty into conviction through systematic inquiry.

memes.label: dr-emerson
memes.description: On the morning of the fire's discovery, Emerson is among the party that hastens to the smouldering ruins of Starvel alongside Sergeant Kent and [[oxley|Mr.

memes.label: dr-lonsdale
memes.description: The coroner demonstrates independent judgment and fairness throughout the proceedings.

memes.label: dr-philpot
memes.description: As Inspector French's investigation deepens, Philpot emerges as a figure of considerable interest.

memes.label: Duty
memes.description: Moral and professional obligation in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, a recurring theme that drives the actions of investigators, witnesses, and citizens throughout the novel.

memes.label: Edinburgh
memes.description: Edinburgh is the capital city of Scotland and a key transit point in French's investigation northward.

memes.label: Death of Edna Philpot
memes.description: The suspicious fatal fall of Edna Philpot down the stairs at Braeside, Kintilloch, in May 1921 — staged by Dr. Philpot as an accident but later revealed as attempted murder

memes.label: evidence
memes.description: In the context of The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, evidence encompasses the physical traces, witness testimony, and documentary records through which Inspector French reconstructs the truth of the Starvel fire.

memes.label: false-identity
memes.description: A false identity is a fabricated persona adopted to conceal one's true identity, typically for criminal purposes or self-protection.

memes.label: Fear
memes.description: Fear as an emotional driver in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy — Ruth's fear of the Hollow, Philpot's dread of exposure, and the pervasive atmosphere of apprehension.

memes.label: Fire as Narrative Engine in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy
aliases: Fire and Destruction as Plot Device

memes.label: forgery
memes.description: Forgery is the criminal act of creating a false document, signature, or other writing with intent to deceive.

memes.label: Fraud
memes.description: The deliberate financial deception at the heart of The Starvel Hollow Tragedy — an elaborate arson-for-insurance scheme involving body substitution, forged documents, and systematic manipulation of banking records.

memes.label: Fraud and Deception in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy
The Investigation Method of Inspector Joseph French
French Baits His Trap
memes.description: Inspector French's stratagem to capture John Roper by luring him to Waverley Station, Edinburgh, using a forged telegram from a fictitious stockbroking firm

memes.label: general-post-office
memes.description: The General Post Office (GPO) was the British government department responsible for postal and telecommunications services.

memes.label: Greed
memes.description: Financial avarice as criminal motive in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy — Roper's and Philpot's insatiable greed driving the arson-murder-fraud scheme.

memes.label: guilt
memes.description: Guilt is the state of having committed a crime or moral wrong, and the attendant psychological condition of awareness of that wrongdoing.

memes.label: Handwriting
memes.description: Forensic handwriting analysis in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy — the examination of forged documents that reveals John Roper's systematic manipulation of letters and confessions, transforming Inspector French's understanding of the case.

memes.label: helen-palmer-gore
memes.description: During Inspector French's investigation, the Palmer-Gore invitation becomes a central point of inquiry.

memes.label: home-office
memes.description: The Home Office is the British government department responsible for immigration, security, and law and order.

memes.label: "Imprint"
memes.description: "Publication imprint and credits"

memes.label: Inquest
memes.description: The formal coroner's inquiry in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy — the legal proceeding where evidence is first presented, witnesses testify, and the gap between official finding and underlying truth is established.

memes.label: insurance-fraud
memes.description: Insurance fraud in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy is the deliberate destruction of property and falsification of death to obtain insurance payouts.

memes.label: investigation
memes.description: In The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, investigation refers to the systematic process of criminal inquiry conducted by Inspector Joseph French of the Criminal Investigation Department at New Scotland Yard.

memes.label: Isolation
memes.description: Physical and psychological isolation in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy — the remote moorland setting of Starvel that enables crime and the emotional solitude that shapes Ruth Averill's character and vulnerability.

memes.label: john-roper
memes.description: John Roper is the male attendant at Starvel, employed along with his wife Flora as live-in servants for Simon Averill.

memes.label: joseph-french
memes.description: Detective Inspector Joseph French is the protagonist of The Starvel Hollow Tragedy and the central investigator sent from New Scotland Yard to unravel the mystery of the fire at Starvel.

memes.label: Jury
memes.description: The coroner's jury in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, its role in the inquest proceedings, and Inspector French's strategic assessment of juries as arbiters of criminal proof.

memes.label: kintilloch
memes.description: Kintilloch is a small town in Fifeshire, Scotland, situated about a mile from the Ransome Institute, the large mental hospital that dominates the local economy and community [006-010].

memes.label: Leeds
memes.description: "Leeds, the major Yorkshire city that serves as the regional hub for the Starvel Hollow investigation — home to Chief Constable Valentine's headquarters, Pierce Whymper's architectural firm and family, Ruth Averill's school, and the source of Simon Averill's property income."

memes.label: London
memes.description: London is the capital of England and the headquarters of the Criminal Investigation Department at New Scotland Yard, where Inspector Joseph French is based.

memes.label: major-valentine
memes.description: Major Valentine serves as the Chief Constable of the County and is a key figure in the escalation of the Starvel Hollow investigation.

memes.label: markham-giles
memes.description: Markham Giles is a semi-invalid entomologist who lives alone in a small cottage half a mile from Starvel, across the moor [001-012].

memes.label: marylebone-station
memes.description: Marylebone Station is a central London railway terminus.

memes.label: metropolitan-safe-deposit-company
memes.description: The Metropolitan Safe Deposit Co., Ltd., located at 25b King William Street in the City of London, is the company where John Roper rented a small safe in their strongrooms.

memes.label: miser
memes.description: A miser is a person who hoards wealth and spends as little money as possible, often to the point of self-deprivation and neglect of others.

memes.label: motive
memes.description: In The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, motive refers to the reason or reasons that drive a person to commit a criminal act.

memes.label: murder
memes.description: In The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, murder is the central crime that the detection narrative works to uncover.

memes.label: northern-shires-bank
memes.description: The Northern Shires Bank is a fictional financial institution headquartered in Throgmorton Avenue, London.

memes.label: Oath
memes.description: Sworn testimony and legal oath-taking in the Starvel Hollow investigation; also the instinctive exclamatory oath as revelation

memes.label: Opportunity
memes.description: One element of the means-motive-opportunity triad in criminal investigation; a recurring narrative mechanism in the Starvel Hollow case

memes.label: oxley
memes.description: As Simon Averill's solicitor, Oxley had drawn up the late man's will and is able to reassure Ruth that "she was not to worry as to her future" and that there would be some money coming to her [196-196].

memes.label: oxley-and-co
memes.description: As guardian: After the fire, Mr.

memes.label: passport-office
memes.description: The Passport Office is the British government agency responsible for issuing passports.

memes.label: peveril-hotel
memes.description: The Peveril Hotel is a hotel in Russell Square, Bloomsbury, London.

memes.label: pierce-whymper
memes.description: Pierce Whymper is a junior assistant to an ecclesiastical architect, serving as clerk of works during renovations to Thirsby parish church. He becomes romantically involved with Ruth Averill and is manipulated by John Roper into an unwitting role in the Starvel Hollow fraud scheme.

memes.label: police-gazette
memes.description: The Police Gazette is the official publication of British police forces, circulated to every police officer in the country.

memes.label: princes-street-station
memes.description: Princes Street Station was a railway station at the west end of Princes Street, Edinburgh.

memes.label: Proof
memes.description: The evidentiary standard in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, where Inspector French's method of cumulative circumstantial evidence transforms suspicion into certainty and certainty into demonstrable proof.

memes.label: Property
memes.description: Property as a motive and investigative focus in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, from Averill's Leeds land holdings to the stolen assets that drive the novel's criminal plot.

memes.label: ransome-institute
memes.description: The Ransome Institute is a large mental hospital situated about a mile from Kintilloch, a small town in Fifeshire, Scotland [006-010].

memes.label: Receipt
memes.description: Documentary receipts in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy as financial evidence, and Inspector French's methodical use of receipts to trace suspects and transactions.

memes.label: Reputation
memes.description: Reputation as a social currency in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy — from professional standing to the criminals' constructed facade of respectability.

memes.label: robbery
memes.description: In The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, robbery is the primary criminal motive underlying the Starvel fire.

memes.label: ruth-averill
memes.description: Ruth Averill is the protagonist of The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, a young orphan dependent on her uncle Simon Averill, whose absence from Starvel during the catastrophic fire places her at the center of the novel's mystery.

memes.label: Scheme
memes.description: Roper's elaborate criminal scheme encompassing arson, murder, fraud, and body substitution at Starvel Hollow — "one of the most diabolical plots ever conceived in the mind of a criminal"

memes.label: scotland-yard
memes.description: Scotland Yard — formally the Criminal Investigation Department of New Scotland Yard — is the detective headquarters of the Metropolitan Police in London and the institutional home of Detective Inspector Joseph French.

memes.label: Secret
memes.description: The role of secrets as a narrative engine in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, from Averill's family secret to Roper's criminal concealments and the investigative imperative to uncover hidden truths.

memes.label: sergeant-kent
memes.description: Sergeant Kent is a police officer with the Thirsby Constabulary and one of the first officials on the scene after the Starvel fire.

memes.label: simon-averill
memes.description: Simon Averill is the owner of Starvel, an old house situated in a hollow on the Yorkshire moors.

memes.label: Simon Averill's Backstory
memes.description: The life, character, and mysterious fate of Simon Averill, the miser of Starvel Hollow — uncle of Ruth Averill and one of the three victims of the fire

memes.label: starvel
memes.description: Starvel is an old, dilapidated house situated at the bottom of Starvel Hollow, a saucer-shaped depression in the moorland of Yorkshire.

memes.label: starvel-hollow-fire
memes.description: On the night of 15 September, Starvel — the isolated house in the Yorkshire moorland hollow — was destroyed by a fire of extraordinary ferocity.

memes.label: strand-palace-hotel
memes.description: The Strand Palace Hotel is a hotel on the Strand in central London.

memes.label: Suicide
memes.description: Self-inflicted death as a narrative element in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, appearing in backstory and as a speculative possibility during the investigation.

memes.label: suspicion
memes.description: In The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, suspicion is the intermediate cognitive state between the observation of anomalous facts and the formation of a definite theory of the crime.

memes.label: Talloires
memes.description: Talloires is a small village on the eastern shore of Lac d'Annecy in the Savoy region of France, rather more than halfway down the lake.

memes.label: tarkington
memes.description: Tarkington's significance emerges from his intimate knowledge of Simon Averill's financial affairs.

memes.label: testimony
memes.description: Testimony is a formal statement given under oath, typically in a legal proceeding such as a trial or inquest.

memes.label: the-inquest
memes.description: The inquest into the deaths of Simon Averill, John Roper, and Flora Roper was held at the Thirsby courthouse on the afternoon of 17 September, two days after the discovery of the Starvel fire.

memes.label: The Yellow Clay Clue
memes.description: The discovery of a small scrap of yellow clay on a handcart wheel at Starvel Hollow, which led Inspector French to uncover the body of Markham Giles buried on the moor

memes.label: thirsby
memes.description: Thirsby is a market town in Yorkshire, situated near Starvel Hollow and serving as the commercial, administrative, and social hub for the rural district in which the novel is set.

memes.label: thirsby-police
memes.description: The Thirsby police force is the local law enforcement authority responsible for the investigation of the Starvel Hollow fire.

memes.label: thirsdale-arms
memes.description: The Thirsdale Arms is the principal hotel in Thirsby, described as "a low, straggling, old-fashioned building with half-timbered gables and a real old swinging sign" [004-007].

memes.label: thomas-cook-and-son
memes.description: The Cook's office enters the investigation when Inspector French, tracing the £20 note paid into the Northern Shires Bank, visits their Regent Street premises.

memes.label: Trap
memes.description: Inspector French's strategic use of baited traps as an investigative technique in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, and the criminals' constant fear of falling into them.

memes.label: Truth
memes.description: The pursuit and elusiveness of truth in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, where Inspector French employs strategic indirection to uncover facts that witnesses and suspects conceal.

memes.label: "Uncopyright"
memes.description: "Public domain declaration"

memes.label: Verdict
memes.description: The formal legal conclusions rendered at the two inquests in The Starvel Hollow Tragedy, from accidental death to wilful murder.

memes.label: waverley-station
memes.description: Waverley Station (also known as Edinburgh Waverley) is the principal railway station in Edinburgh, Scotland.

memes.label: witness
memes.description: A witness is a person who gives evidence under oath in a legal proceeding, testifying to facts within their personal knowledge.

memes.label: york
memes.description: York is a historic cathedral city in Yorkshire, England, and the destination of Ruth Averill's visit at the time of the Starvel fire.
